title: Handle 7z multi-volume archives (.7z.NNN split sets)
description: 7z multi-volume sets (name.7z.001 ... name.7z.NNN) are skipped entirely: the file extension is .001/.002/etc (not .7z), so no volume is recognized as an archive and the whole set is missed — not tagged, not staged, silently absent from the CSV. Observed: Grey Knights.7z.001-006, Pirate Orc Boy Pack_2.7z.001-002, Plague Infantry.7z.001+, all 0 rows in the CSV. (Distinct from .partN.rar which over-produced; this under-produces.) patoolib extracts the .001 first volume fine (detects 7z by content, 7z pulls the siblings). Fix: generalize multi-volume handling — multipart_volume_number also matches .(7z|zip|rar).(NNN); strip_multipart_suffix drops the trailing .7z/.zip/.rar exposed in the stem (Grey Knights.7z -> Grey Knights); tag discovery treats .7z.001 as the taggable first volume (skips .002+); extract_to_temp and stage_into_library route split first-volumes to patoolib. Add tests for 7z split discovery + staging.
close_reason: Implemented alongside .partN.rar in PR #47: split .7z.NNN sets handled (first volume only, .ext.NNN stripped, continuations skipped)

title: Handle multi-part RAR archives as one model (.partN.rar volumes)
description:
Multi-volume RAR sets (name.part1.rar/name.part01.rar ... name.partN.rar) are walked as independent files: the first volume extracts the whole set (tagged), while continuation volumes extract partial/garbage content — sometimes 'tagged' with wrong data, sometimes 'no valid content' errors. A 7-part 'Krieg' set becomes up to 7 separate models. Observed 33 .partN.rar rows incl. continuations (Krieg.part03, Chaos Warlord.part02, ...).

Fix: treat a .partN.rar set as ONE model. unrar pulls the whole set from the first volume (siblings live alongside in --zips), so: (1) tag discovery skips continuation volumes (part number != 1); (2) strip the .partN marker from derived names so the model is 'Krieg' not 'Krieg part01' (in utils.clean_file_name + manyfold_ingest._model_dir_name); (3) run_upload skips continuation-volume rows defensively; (4) the tag resume drops stale continuation rows so existing CSVs self-heal. Add utils helpers multipart_volume_number / strip_multipart_suffix. Note: 'Tyranid Warriors Part 3.rar' (space, single .rar) is NOT a volume and must be unaffected.

Follow-up (separate): --delete-source should remove all sibling volumes of a set, not just the first.
close_reason: Implemented: multi-part RAR sets handled as one model (first volume only, .partN stripped, continuations skipped in tag+upload, resume self-heals)

title: _flatten_into_root: cap flattened filename length to avoid ENAMETOOLONG
description: _flatten_into_root names each flattened file '_'.join(rel.parts), i.e. the full nested path joined by underscores. For deeply nested archives this exceeds the filesystem 255-byte per-component limit and raises [Errno 63] File name too long, so the whole archive fails to stage. Observed: General/Chubby Pattern Contender... produced a 200+ char name. Fix: when the joined name exceeds a safe length (~200 bytes), shorten it while preserving the suffix and keeping uniqueness — e.g. truncate the joined stem and append a short hash of the relative path. Add a test with a deeply nested member whose joined name exceeds the limit, asserting the staged file exists with a capped, unique name and the suffix preserved.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #45: client re-auths on 401, flatten caps overlong names

title: ManyfoldClient: refresh OAuth token on 401 (long runs expire the cached token)
description: _ensure_token caches self._token and never refreshes; _request retries only on 429/5xx, not 401. Manyfold's OAuth access token (Doorkeeper, ~2h default TTL) expires during long runs, so all API calls after expiry fail with 'HTTP 401: Failed to Login'. Observed on a 4h49m full upload: every matched-model get_model/update after ~2.5h failed (General/Execrator, General/Cato Sicarius, Finished scans/Execrator, Finished scans/Cato Sicarius). Fix: in _request, on a 401 response, clear self._token, call _ensure_token() to re-auth, and retry the request once. Add a test: first call 401s, client re-auths and retries, second succeeds.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #45: client re-auths on 401, flatten caps overlong names

title: stage_into_library: flatten archive contents so Manyfold creates one model per zip
description:
Manyfold treats each folder containing 3D files as a single model and each SUBFOLDER as a separate model (confirmed via Manyfold docs/maintainer: manyfold.app/manual/scanning.html, discussions/5020 — no setting controls this; only a manual merge feature exists). stage_into_library extracts each archive into a folder with internal subfolders (Supported/, Large Printers/, Body/, ...), so a single zip fans out into many models, none of which receive our datapackage.json tags (they end up with only Manyfold's '!new' auto-tag). Observed: a 257-row run produced 826+ untagged, uncollected models.

Fix: in stage_into_library, after extraction, flatten every file under the staging dir into the staging root (no subfolders) with collision-safe names derived from the file's relative path (separators -> '_'), then write datapackage.json at the root. Manyfold then sees one folder = one model with all parts at its root, and the datapackage tags apply.

Add a test (mock patoolib.extract_archive to write a nested tree incl. a same-basename collision across subfolders) asserting the staged dest is flat (no subdirs), parts are present with collision-safe names, the root-level files are preserved, and datapackage.json sits at the root with the tags.

Caveat for rollout: existing staged folders on disk short-circuit via 'if dest.exists()'; to re-stage flat, the old folders in MANYFOLD_LIBRARY_PATH must be removed (user is deleting the bad models manually).
close_reason: Implemented: stage_into_library flattens extracted archive contents into one flat folder so Manyfold creates one model per zip

title: Recursive --zips: tag + upload must handle nested folders
description:
Both tag and upload assume model archives sit directly in --zips, so a nested
incoming library is mostly missed.

- tag: src/tagging.py uses Path(zips_dir).iterdir() (non-recursive) — archives
  in subfolders are never tagged.
- upload: src/manyfold_ingest.py uses source = Path(zips_dir) / filename (flat),
  and the CSV stores only path.name (basename), so nested sources aren't found
  and same-basename files in different subfolders collide on one CSV row.

Fix: tag recurses with rglob and writes the path RELATIVE to --zips as the CSV
filename (recursive + collision-free); upload joins that relative path onto
--zips to locate the source. match_model and _model_dir_name already use
Path(...).stem/.name (final component), so naming/matching keep working with a
relative-path filename. Add tests for nested discovery, relative-path CSV, and
collision avoidance.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #41: tag recurses with rglob and writes relative-path CSV filenames; upload re-joins onto --zips

title: Tune weak-context: skip warning when slug filter fired; raise unfiltered threshold to MiniLM scale (~0.8)
Embed resume: skip documents already embedded in the target collection
Requeue pages on transient Wayback failure instead of skipping (capped retries)
Skip non-article namespace URLs at scrape time (seeds + discovered links)
Bulk CDX snapshot index: one-time domain prefetch with resume-key pagination, disk cache
Match scanned Manyfold models to CSV rows; apply namespaced tags (update owned namespaces, preserve manual tags)
Manyfold API client module: token auth, pagination, rate limiting, retry/backoff
Upload transport: folder-drop into library + scan trigger, HTTP multipart upload as fallback
Spike: verify Manyfold API against live instance (model list/create, tag update, collections, scan trigger, auth)
Feed retrieved page titles + wiki categories to the LLM context
Structured tag output: JSON schema with canonical faction/role vocab, CSV columns
Skip non-article wiki namespaces (File:/Category:/...) at embed time
Retrieval: rank-delta doc selection + per-page diversity cap
Retrieval: n-gram slug lookup replacing case-sensitive $contains
Embedder-aware chunk sizing (default EF truncates at 256 wordpieces)
Embed-time dedupe of redirect-duplicate articles by title
Embed-time title repair for generic site titles (derive from URL)
Embed-time markdown cleanup: banner tables, TOC, footnote refs, boilerplate sections
Convert main.py to subcommand CLI (scrape/embed/tag/upload)
Rewrite README to match actual CLI flags, markdown output, Wayback/resume behavior
Fix scraper thread-safety: visited/results races and save_progress clearing
Fix rotten filtered flag and superset tie-overwrite in tagging.py retrieval
Guard empty retrieval in tagging.py (IndexError on empty vector DB)
Wire --prompt-override through main.py to run_tagging
--faction: pin the faction column for single-faction libraries
description:
PROBLEM
For a single-faction library (e.g. /Volumes/Media1/Tyranids Complete), the LLM still guesses faction per file and gets a few wrong (proxy names -> Orks/Drukhari, signal-less part archives -> hallucinated factions) or blank. The only fix today is manual CSV editing or a full --prompt-override.

GOAL
A --faction VALUE flag on 'tag' that deterministically sets the faction column on every row (the LLM still determines unit/model_type/role/tags). Guarantees no faction outliers/blanks and everything collects under one faction on upload. Reusable for any single-faction dump.

FIX
- main.py: add --faction to the tag options; pass to run_tagging.
- tagging.py run_tagging(faction=None): if set, validate the mode has a 'faction' field (else SystemExit); warn if the value isn't in the schema enum; after normalize/parse, set parsed['faction']=value before writing each row.

TESTS
- run_tagging with faction='Tyranids' writes faction=Tyranids on every row regardless of what the model returned
- --faction on a mode without a faction field errors
- other fields (unit, tags) still come from the model

title: loose-as-folders: variant denylist misses abbreviations (stl supp / lys supp / stl fin)
description:
PROBLEM
GUARD 1's variant/wrapper detection (_is_variant_folder) only matches exact single words {obj,stl,raw,files,lychee,chitubox,lys} and the substrings supported/unsupported/presupported. Compound/abbreviated format+prep folder names slip through: 'stl supp', 'lys supp', 'stl fin', 'stl fixed' are treated as REAL model-bearing children -> the parent SPLITS into them, and each sub-unit loses the parent's name.

Observed on /Volumes/Media1/Tyranids Complete: 'Neurogants' split into 'stl supp' / 'lys supp' / 'stl fin'. The 'stl supp' unit had no name signal -> LLM hallucinated 'Canoptek Spyder' and tagged it Necrons (in a pure Tyranid library).

FIX
Treat a folder whose name is made up ENTIRELY of format/print-prep tokens as a variant/wrapper folder (fold into parent), via a token check: split the name (loose_folders._tokens) and if every non-digit token is a variant/format word (obj/stl/lys/raw/files/supported/unsupported/supp/sup/fin/final/fixed/hollow/solid/merged/cut/ready/print/version/...), it's a variant folder. Keeps 'Support Weapon' (weapon != variant) and 'Genestealer 1' as real models.

Effect: Neurogants -> variant-only -> ONE model 'Neurogants' (all format variants flattened in), tagged Tyranids from the name.

TESTS
- _is_variant_folder true for 'stl supp','lys supp','stl fin','STL Fixed'; false for 'Support Weapon','Barbgants','Genestealer 1'
- a folder with only 'stl supp'/'lys supp'/'stl fin' children resolves to ONE unit named after the folder (not a split)
close_reason: Implemented in PR #57, CI green; awaiting user merge

title: loose-as-folders merges archive-collection folders (direct-files-win pulls in archives)
description:
PROBLEM
resolve_model_units 'direct files win' keys on loose MODEL_EXTS only and ignores archives for boundary detection, but staging copytrees the WHOLE folder. A folder that is mostly ARCHIVES with a few stray loose files therefore collapses into ONE model that merges all the archives. Observed on AkroniusScrapheaps: 'KUH Niggets and BIGGUNs' = 132 archives + 67 loose -> --loose-as-folders yields 1 unit merging 132 distinct minis. Every top folder there is an archive collection, so the feature is unusable on that library.

This is the 'folders mixing loose files + archives' edge flagged as residual risk in ModelTagger2-17z.

FIX (decide)
- If a folder contains archives, do NOT let stray loose files trigger direct-files-win / a merged unit; archives stay one-model-each (existing archive discovery), and only genuinely archive-free loose subtrees are grouped.
- Or: exclude archive-bearing folders from loose grouping entirely (warn), leaving them to normal per-archive staging.
- Add an archive-share guard so an archive-dominated folder is never merged.

TESTS
- a folder with N archives + a stray .stl -> archives stay N models, stray file handled separately (not one merged blob)
- a pure loose-file kit still groups as before (files/ example unchanged)
close_reason: Implemented (PR #55 / #56), CI pending; awaiting user merge

title: prune subcommand: delete Manyfold models by exact-name list
description:
PROBLEM
Cleanup requires deleting many junk/stray models (e.g. 219 render-images + stray loose bits staged from AkroniusScrapheaps, see found_fragments.txt). The client can list/update/create but has NO delete, so removing them means hand-deleting hundreds in the Manyfold UI.

GOAL
A safe bulk-delete driven by an exact-name list.

FIX
- Add ManyfoldClient.delete_model (DELETE on the model resource path).
- Add run_prune + a 'prune' subcommand: read a names file (one per line), match EXACTLY (case-insensitive) against Manyfold model name/title, delete matches.
- Safety: skip names matching 0 models (not_found) or >1 (ambiguous -> report, never guess); hold back short/generic names (len<4 or denylist) unless --allow-generic; --dry-run prints the plan; --limit caps.

TESTS
- delete_model issues DELETE to the resource path
- run_prune deletes exact matches, skips not_found + ambiguous, holds generic, dry-run deletes nothing, --limit caps, --allow-generic includes short names
close_reason: Implemented in PR #54 (feat/prune-models), CI pending; awaiting user merge

title: Assign faction collections from each model's own tags, not CSV name-matching
description:
PROBLEM
run_upload assigns the faction collection only in the matched-model branch, linking CSV rows to Manyfold models by normalize_name. At scale this is fragile: observed 3253 models -> only 2876 distinct normalize_name slugs (377 collisions, so the name->model dict drops duplicates and they're unreachable) and 441 faction CSV rows with no exact model match (the staged model's name from _model_dir_name normalizes differently than the CSV filename). Result: ~570 models that HAVE a 'faction: X' tag are left with no collection (Unassigned). (Separately, ~550 are blank-faction and correctly unassigned.)

INSIGHT
Every staged model already carries its faction as a namespaced tag ('faction: <value>') written into datapackage.json at stage time. So the collection can be derived from the model itself — no CSV, no name matching, no collisions.

FIX
Add a reconciliation pass / mode (e.g. 'upload --reconcile-collections', or run it as part of the normal post-scan pass): list all Manyfold models, and for each model that has a 'faction:' owned-namespace tag AND no isPartOf, ensure that faction collection and set isPartOf. Idempotent; honors the keep-manual-collection rule (only assign when isPartOf is empty). This (a) fixes the already-uploaded models and (b) makes future runs robust regardless of name drift/collisions. Generalize to the per-mode collection field (faction for warhammer; terrain_type/etc. for the future terrain mode, ModelTagger2-78x).

ALTERNATIVE (note): stage under a <faction>/ folder + Manyfold 'Infer metadata from path' assigns the collection at scan with no second pass — more elegant but depends on that Manyfold setting and a staging-layout change.

TESTS
- model with faction tag + no isPartOf -> collection ensured + isPartOf set
- model already in a collection -> left untouched (keep-manual)
- model with no faction tag (blank) -> skipped
- collection created once per faction (cached)
close_reason: Implemented in PR #51 (feat/reconcile-collections), CI green; awaiting user merge

title: Add --mode terrain: terrain taxonomy preset + per-mode collection field
description:
PROBLEM
The warhammer/dnd presets are character-centric (faction/subfaction/unit/model_type/role/allegiance/equipment). Terrain (buildings, ruins, bunkers, turrets, pipes, containers, walkways, objectives) doesn't fit: those enums come out 'unknown'/garbage, and the unit-lore RAG is irrelevant (weak-context guard just discards it). Example folder /Volumes/Media1/FOLDER1/_Terrain = ~513 archives (Sanctum Imperialis, Necron pylons, VMT bunkers/towers/generators, modular pipes, shipping containers, Zone Mortalis doors, damaged ruins, defence lines) + a Terrain_unsorted/ subfolder.

GOAL
A new --mode terrain preset in config/tagging_presets.json with a terrain taxonomy, so terrain pieces get useful tags and land cleanly in Manyfold alongside minis.

PROPOSED SCHEMA (lock enum lists before build)
- terrain_type (enum): building, ruin, fortification, wall/barricade, tower, bunker, gate/door, bridge/walkway, platform, emplacement/turret, industrial (pipes/containers/generators), objective, scatter, monument
- setting (enum): sci-fi/industrial, gothic-imperial, xenos, urban-ruins (Zone Mortalis/Necromunda), fantasy/medieval, modern, wasteland
- faction_theme (nullable enum, reuse the 24 factions): for themed terrain (Sanctum->Imperium, Pylon->Necrons); blank for generic
- function (enum): los-blocker, cover/defensible, objective, decorative/scatter, gameplay (door/spawn)
- modular (bool/yes-no): tile/modular systems (Modular Pipes, Conduit Pack)
- tags (array): free-form

RETRIEVAL
Terrain categorizes from the filename + model knowledge; no unit-lore RAG needed. Run with retrieval effectively disabled (empty/tiny vector_db OR a skip-retrieval path in run_tagging, which currently always queries the DB). Decide which.

SHARED (no change)
All file handling reused: extract/flatten, multi-volume (.partN.rar / .7z.NNN), nested archives (ModelTagger2-s5y), loose-folder (ModelTagger2-17z). Terrain is ~513 archives + Terrain_unsorted/.

INTEGRATION POINTS (upload)
- manyfold_ingest.OWNED_NAMESPACES is already a cross-mode union (has dnd fields) -> add the terrain fields so terrain_type:/setting:/faction_theme: etc. namespace + update-owned-keep-manual works.
- The Manyfold collection is currently hard-keyed to 'faction'. Make the collection field PER-MODE: terrain collects by terrain_type (or setting), not faction. This is the main cross-cutting change.

OPEN ITEMS TO LOCK BEFORE BUILD
- exact enum lists for terrain_type/setting/function
- retrieval: skip path vs empty DB
- collection field for terrain (terrain_type vs setting)
- keep faction_theme? (themed vs generic terrain)

TESTS
- terrain preset loads; schema drives CSV columns
- a terrain filename tags into terrain_type/setting (not faction/unit)
- upload namespaces terrain fields and collects by the per-mode field
- file handling unchanged for terrain archives
close_reason: Implemented in PR #50 (feat/terrain-mode), CI green; awaiting user merge

title: Handle nested archives (archive containing inner .zip/.rar/.7z with models inside)
description:
PROBLEM
A common Patreon repack: an outer archive (e.g. Bloody Angelic Master.rar) contains only INNER archives (.zip/.rar/.7z), one per sub-model, with the actual .stl/.obj one level deeper. The pipeline extracts only ONE level, so is_valid_archive_content sees only ARCHIVE_EXTS files (no model files) -> 'could not extract / no valid content' -> the archive is SKIPPED and its models are silently lost.

Observed in AkroniusScrapheaps:
- Bloody Angelic Master.rar -> contains 2 .zip
- Intercessor and Assault Intercessor.rar -> 2 .rar
- Norn Emissary P1.rar -> 5 .zip
(likely many more 'no valid content' skips in that folder are this.)

FIX
After extracting an archive, if the extracted tree contains nested archives (and no/again-after model files), recursively extract those too before validating/flattening, so is_valid_archive_content sees the real model files, tagging proceeds, and stage_into_library flattens everything into ONE model (one outer archive = one model, consistent with current behavior).

CONSIDERATIONS
- Bounded recursion depth (e.g. <=2-3 levels) to avoid archive bombs / runaway nesting; stop if no progress.
- Security: still reject if ANY extracted file at ANY level is a BAD_EXTS executable.
- Apply on BOTH sides: tagging (extract_to_temp + is_valid_archive_content) and upload (stage_into_library must unpack nested archives before _flatten_into_root).
- Inner archives may themselves be multi-volume/split or corrupt -> handle gracefully (skip the bad one, keep the rest).
- Default = one model per OUTER archive (flatten all nested content in). Treating each inner archive as its own model is the separate loose-files/folder-as-model discussion (ModelTagger2-17z) - out of scope here.

TESTS
- outer archive -> inner zip -> stl: tagged + staged as ONE model
- nested archive containing an executable: rejected
- depth cap respected; corrupt inner archive skipped but siblings still extracted
close_reason: Implemented: extract_nested_archives recursively unpacks inner .zip/.rar/.7z bundles in tag + upload, executable-safe, bounded depth

title: Loose-files mode: folder-as-model with descend/split boundary detection
description:
PROBLEM
Loose model files in a directory tree (no archive) are each tagged/staged as their own model by the recursive discovery, exploding a kit into dozens of models + LLM calls. Example: ./files/ (Barbgants + Biovore, 57 loose .stl, 0 archives) -> 57 models + 57 LLM calls today; desired is 5.

GOAL (opt-in, e.g. --loose-as-folders)
Detect model boundaries for loose-file folders so a folder tree becomes the right number of models, then tag once per model and stage each (flattened) as one Manyfold model. Archives are unaffected (still one model each); the --zips root is just the container (a loose file directly in the root stays its own model, as today).

RULE (validated on ./files/)
Starting from each immediate child of --zips, for a folder:
1. If it DIRECTLY contains model files -> it is ONE model; flatten its whole subtree into it (stop descending). 'Direct files win'.
2. Else if it has exactly ONE model-bearing subfolder -> descend (wrapper collapse), keeping the current model name.
3. Else (multiple model-bearing subfolders, no direct files) -> SPLIT: each such subfolder is its own model; recurse.
Model NAME = the top of the collapsed single-child chain (e.g. Barbgants, Biovore 3). Needs a fallback to the deepest meaningful folder name when the top is generic (Patreon / files / creator name).

WORKED EXAMPLE (./files/ -> 5 models)
- Barbgants/KaiTech_Design/space-bug.../ (+/Supported) -> 1 model 'Barbgants' (wrapper chain collapses; Supported folds in)
- Biovore/ has 4 model-bearing children -> split:
  - Bios Voreus (+ Spore_mine_with_base) -> 'Bios Voreus'
  - Biovore -> 'Biovore'
  - Biovore 2 (+ bit subfolders) -> 'Biovore 2'
  - Biovore 3/files/ -> 'Biovore 3'

INTEGRATION
- tag discovery: when --loose-as-folders, resolve loose folders to model units via the rule; CSV filename = the folder path relative to --zips (e.g. 'Biovore/Biovore 2'); one row + one LLM call per unit.
- upload: Path(zips)/<folder> -> copy/flatten the folder subtree into one staged model (reuse _flatten_into_root + datapackage). _model_dir_name on a folder path -> the chain-top name.
- Default behavior (no flag) unchanged.

JUDGMENT CALLS (confirmed with user)
- 'Direct files win': a folder with its own STLs is one model even if it also has model-bearing subfolders (folds in supported/unsupported + bits). Accepted.
- Name from chain top; add deepest-meaningful fallback for generic tops.

TESTS
- boundary detection on a synthetic tree mirroring files/ (wrapper-collapse -> 1; grouping-split -> N; direct-files-win folds sub-bits in; root container)
- tag produces one row per model unit keyed by folder path
- upload stages each unit flattened as one model named after the chain top

NOTE: design validated only on ./files/ (Barbgants/Biovore). User is gathering more loose folders to test before build; re-validate edge cases (generic top names, folders mixing loose files + archives, deeper grouping) against those before/while implementing.
design:
REFINEMENTS validated by simulating against ./files/ (20 Necron HQ chars in 01. HQ/, 02. Troops/ with Warriors OBJ+STL variants, Tyranid Barbgants/Biovore, and a Hive Tyrant kitbash). Pure descend/split rule gave 58 models; with the two guards below it gives 31 — every one a real sculpt. Apply BOTH guards when deciding whether to split a folder's model-bearing children.

GUARD 1 — variant/wrapper-folder denylist (don't count these as separate models; their files flatten into the parent):
  exact (case-insensitive): obj, stl, raw, files, lychee, chitubox, lys
  substring match: supported, unsupported, presupported, pre-supported, lychee, chitubox
  Effect: e.g. Warriors (8E)/{OBJ,STL} -> one model 'Warriors (8E)' (OBJ+STL flattened); Biovore 3/files -> 'Biovore 3'.

GUARD 2 — parts-kitbash heuristic (a folder whose children are mostly part-named is ONE model, not a split):
  A folder with >=3 model-bearing children where >=60% are 'part-like' -> one model (flatten the whole subtree), don't split.
  part-like = any token (split on whitespace/_/-/./digit-boundary) is in PART_WORDS:
  body bodies head heads arm arms hand hands leg legs base bases bust torso wing wings gun guns weapon weapons sword swords saber blade blades claw claws tail tails helmet shoulder shoulders backpack cape cloak horn horns spine mandible mandibles thorax pose part parts rending gland glands hook hooks spike spikes plate plates
  Effect: Hive Tyrant and winged/ (19 part folders: base, saber, fuhrer_sword, rending_claws_*, wings, ...) -> one model 'Hive Tyrant and winged' (100 parts), instead of 19 fragments.

EVALUATION ORDER per folder: (1) direct model files -> one model; else (2) drop variant folders from the child set; (3) if no real children left -> one model (variant-only); (4) if parts-kitbash -> one model; (5) if exactly 1 real child -> wrapper-collapse (descend, keep name); (6) else -> split into the real children, recurse.

RESIDUAL RISK (name-based): a kitbash whose parts have proper names could slip through (-> over-split), and a genuine multi-sculpt folder full of part-words could wrongly merge (-> under-split). MITIGATION: add a MAX-SPLIT guard — if a single folder would explode into N (>~8) tiny models, don't auto-commit; flag it for a quick human glance/override. Treat the denylist + PART_WORDS as tunable; revisit as more libraries are tested.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #53 (feat/loose-as-folders), CI green; awaiting user merge

title: Support all Manyfold file formats (3D mesh, slicer/chitubox, images) in tag + upload
description: The pipeline only recognizes .stl/.obj/.png (+ .zip/.rar/.7z archives), so archives/loose files in formats Manyfold supports (e.g. .chitubox/.ctb/.lys slicer projects, .3mf/.ply/.step meshes) are skipped as 'no valid content' and never tagged or staged. Match Manyfold's supported set (manyfold.app/manual/supported_formats.html). Centralize the extension lists in utils.py (MESH/SLICER/IMAGE/ARCHIVE/MODEL/LOOSE sets) as one source of truth, and use them in: tagging.extract_to_temp (archive vs loose copy), is_valid_archive_content (allowed exts), the discovery loop, and manyfold_ingest _ARCHIVE_EXTS/_LOOSE_EXTS. Keep the executable bad_exts security gate. Loose documents/video/PCB are NOT treated as standalone models (but already ride along inside archives via flatten). Update README formats list. Add tests for new mesh + slicer formats (incl. a chitubox-only archive now tagging).
close_reason: Implemented: centralized Manyfold's supported formats in utils (mesh/slicer/image/archive) and used across tag + upload

title: run_upload: same-basename files in different --zips subfolders collide on one staged folder
description:
Since recursive --zips (relative-path CSV filenames), two different sources sharing a basename (e.g. 'General/Triarch Praetorians.zip' and 'Finished scans/Triarch Praetorians.zip') both stage to library/_model_dir_name(basename) = 'Triarch Praetorians'. The second hits 'if dest.exists(): return dest' and is silently NOT staged — its model never reaches Manyfold. Observed: 5 such pairs in a 257-row run (General/ vs Finished scans/).

Fix: in run_upload, pre-scan CSV filenames and assign each a unique staged-folder name; when a basename collides across rows, disambiguate with the relative parent path (e.g. 'Triarch Praetorians (General)' vs 'Triarch Praetorians (Finished scans)'). Pass the resolved dest name into stage_into_library (new optional dest_name param). Non-colliding names stay clean. This stages BOTH models; each still gets its tags from its own datapackage at scan time.

Known residual (document, don't fix here): on the second upload pass, faction-collection matching is basename-slug based, so same-basename models may match ambiguously — but both are staged and tagged via datapackage regardless.

Tests: _staged_dir_names disambiguates collisions / keeps non-colliding clean; run_upload stages two same-basename sources into two distinct folders.
close_reason: Implemented: _staged_dir_names disambiguates same-basename sources by relative parent so both stage into distinct folders (folded into PR #45)

title: _model_dir_name doesn't dedupe repeated words, producing doubled model titles
description:
Vendor 'doubled name' filenames (e.g. 'Sister Superior_Sister+Superior.zip') produce a doubled model title/folder when staged into Manyfold: _model_dir_name (src/manyfold_ingest.py:98) yields 'Sister Superior Sister Superior', which becomes both the staged folder name and the datapackage.json title/name. Surfaced in Manyfold as a model titled/slugged 'sister-superior-sister-superior'. normalize_name already dedupes repeated words via filter_query_tokens (so match_model works), but _model_dir_name only does regex cleanup with no dedupe, so the human-facing title/folder is doubled.

Fix: dedupe repeated words case-insensitively in _model_dir_name (preserving order and original casing, mirroring filter_query_tokens' seen-set), without dropping digits/apostrophes that the title legitimately needs.

Add a test for the doubled-name -> single-title case (e.g. 'Sister Superior_Sister+Superior.zip' -> 'Sister Superior'), and confirm normal/non-doubled names are unaffected.

Note: this is distinct from the Manyfold 'Path does not match template' problems, which are Manyfold's path-template/Organize-files feature reacting to our flat staged folder vs a tag-nested template -- not a bug in our upload.
close_reason: Fixed: _model_dir_name dedupes repeated words case-insensitively (combined PR)

title: Add tagged_at timestamp column to tagging CSV
description: Record when each file was tagged. Append a tagged_at column (ISO-8601 UTC) to the tagging CSV header and write the current time for each newly-tagged/retried row. Kept rows on resume retain their original timestamp. Resume logic must migrate older CSVs that predate the column by backfilling an empty timestamp instead of forcing a fresh file. Upload reads via DictReader so the extra column is ignored.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #42: trailing tagged_at ISO-8601 UTC column with in-place migration of pre-timestamp CSVs

title: Raise Wayback snapshot fetch timeout 10s->30s (fresh June-2026 captures serve slowly)
Jitter shared-backoff wakeups to stop 503 thundering herd
Committed eval script tools/eval_retrieval.py: repeatable retrieval probe over sample archives
Upload CLI polish: --dry-run report, --limit, resume at thousands-scale; README docs
Faction collections: ensure collection exists per faction, assign models
Re-run retrieval evaluation once the warhammer_lore scrape completes (currently at 'O')
Confidence-aware prompting when retrieval distance is weak
Retrieval: filter junk tokens from filename-derived queries
Heading-aware chunking with section prefixes; embed categories+infobox summary chunk
Update README and tests for new subcommand CLI
Add 'all' pipeline command running scrape→embed→tag
Add lore_dir to tagging presets; resolve all per-mode defaults from config
Add tests for scrape.py (login wall, filenames, progress round-trip)
Crawler: rolling thread window, correct tqdm total, live-site politeness delay
Make embedding idempotent (upsert instead of add)
Pin dependency versions and split heavy local-mode deps; slim CI install
Resolve config path relative to repo; configure logging lazily in tagging.py
Fix temp-dir leak in extract_to_temp on failure paths
Use response.usage for OpenAI token counts; drop hardcoded cost estimate
Lone image files are tagged and staged as their own models
description:
PROBLEM
tag discovery walks TAGGABLE_EXTS, which includes IMAGE_EXTS (via LOOSE_EXTS), so a standalone render (.jpg/.png/.gif) with no accompanying model is tagged AND staged as its own Manyfold model. Observed: 30 render images became models in AkroniusScrapheaps (e.g. 'Lord Castellan Creed...png', 'Army guys' had 120 loose images). These are clutter and land Unassigned.

FIX
Images should only ride along INSIDE an archive/folder model, never be a model on their own. Exclude standalone image files from tag discovery (and from loose-file staging): a loose file is a taggable model only if its ext is in MODEL_EXTS, not merely LOOSE_EXTS. Keep images inside archives (is_valid_archive_content / staging) unchanged.

TESTS
- a lone .png under --zips is not discovered/tagged
- a .stl is still discovered
- an archive containing renders still stages them alongside the model
close_reason: Implemented (PR #55 / #56), CI pending; awaiting user merge

title: Add an Age of Sigmar / Warhammer Fantasy (Old World) faction preset/mode
description:
PROBLEM
Spot-checking the still-Unassigned 40K models showed ~a third are Age of Sigmar / Warhammer Fantasy / Old World minis (Soulblight/Vampires e.g. Prince Vhordrai, Skaven e.g. Stormvermin, Nagash, Dwarfs/Ironbreakers, Tomb Kings/Egypt soldiers). They HAVE real factions, but not in the warhammer-40k preset's faction enum, so the tagger correctly leaves faction blank and they land Unassigned.

GOAL
A new preset/mode (e.g. --mode aos or --mode fantasy) with an AoS + Old World faction taxonomy, so these get factions + collections like 40K minis. Mirrors the existing warhammer/dnd presets and the terrain mode (ModelTagger2-78x).

SCHEMA (faction enum covers both AoS Grand Alliances and the Old World; lock the list before build)
- Order: Stormcast Eternals, Cities of Sigmar, Fyreslayers, Kharadron Overlords, Lumineth Realm-lords, Daughters of Khaine, Idoneth Deepkin, Sylvaneth, Seraphon
- Chaos: Slaves to Darkness, Blades of Khorne, Disciples of Tzeentch, Maggotkin of Nurgle, Hedonites of Slaanesh, Skaven, Beasts of Chaos
- Death: Soulblight Gravelords, Nighthaunt, Ossiarch Bonereapers, Flesh-eater Courts
- Destruction: Orruk Warclans, Gloomspite Gitz, Ogor Mawtribes, Sons of Behemat
- Old World (if combining): Empire/Cities, Bretonnia, Dwarfs, High/Wood/Dark Elves, Tomb Kings, Vampire Counts, Lizardmen, Chaos Warriors, Greenskins, etc.
- plus subfaction, unit, model_type, role (battlefield role), grand_alliance (Order/Chaos/Death/Destruction) as the allegiance analog, equipment, tags
- Decision: one combined fantasy mode vs separate aos/old-world; and whether to canonicalize Old World names onto AoS factions (Vampire Counts -> Soulblight) or keep both.

RETRIEVAL / LORE
The 40K Chroma corpus is irrelevant. Either run retrieval-disabled (filename + model knowledge, like the terrain plan) or scrape/embed an AoS/Fantasy lore corpus (ageofsigmar.lexicanum / warhammer fantasy wiki) into its own --vector-db-path.

INTEGRATION (mostly shared)
- faction stays the  namespace (already in OWNED_NAMESPACES) and collects by faction (same as warhammer) -> upload works unchanged.
- per-mode collection field (from ModelTagger2-78x) applies.
- all file handling reused.

TESTS: preset loads; an AoS/Fantasy filename tags into the AoS faction vocab; upload collects by faction.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #52 (feat/aos-mode), CI green; awaiting user merge

title: Add a progress bar to the tagging loop (tqdm)
description:
run_tagging (src/tagging.py) loops over Path(zips_dir).rglob('*') with no overall progress indication — on a large library a tag run is hours long and you can't tell how far along it is or estimate ETA. The upload step already uses tqdm (run_upload: 'for row in tqdm(rows, desc=Syncing)'); tagging should too.

Implementation:
- Pre-collect the discoverable file list (apply the existing filters once: is_file, suffix in TAGGABLE_EXTS, skip multipart continuation volumes) so tqdm has a real total (percentage + ETA), rather than barring an un-sized generator.
- Wrap the loop in tqdm(files, desc='Tagging'); set the current filename as postfix (bar.set_postfix_str / desc) for context.
- Route the per-file console messages ('Tagged X ...', 'Skipping ...', '[Retry on re-run] ...', 'Error processing ...') through tqdm.write() so they don't corrupt the bar; leave the logging.* calls to tagging.log unchanged.
- Resume: already-processed files are skipped — either keep them in the total (bar jumps quickly past skips) or exclude them so the bar reflects real work; pick the cleaner UX.
- tqdm is already a dependency (used by upload). No new deps.

Tests: existing tagging tests still pass (correctness unchanged). A progress bar is hard/low-value to unit-test; optionally assert run completes and rows are written as before. No behavioral change to the CSV or tags.
close_reason: Built together: delete-source removes all multipart volumes; tqdm progress bar in tagging; corrupt-vs-empty skip-message split + logged extractor error

title: Split the 'could not extract / no valid content' skip message into two cases
description:
run_tagging logs the same 'Skipping X — could not extract / no valid content (will retry on re-run)' for two distinct failures: (a) extraction ERRORED (corrupt/incomplete archive, e.g. a 7z split missing later volumes -> 'Unexpected end of archive'), and (b) extraction SUCCEEDED but produced no taggable model content. These need different user action (re-download vs unsupported/nested content) but log identically, making diagnosis hard.

Fix: distinguish in run_tagging — when extract_to_temp returns None (extraction failed) log 'extraction failed (corrupt/incomplete?)'; when extraction succeeded but is_valid_archive_content is False log 'no model content (unsupported or nested archives)'. Optionally surface the extractor's error text. Small log-only change; pairs with the nested-archive work.
close_reason: Built together: delete-source removes all multipart volumes; tqdm progress bar in tagging; corrupt-vs-empty skip-message split + logged extractor error

title: --delete-source should remove all volumes of a multi-part RAR set
description: With multi-part RAR handling, run_upload stages a set via its first volume (e.g. Krieg.part01.rar) and skips the continuation rows. But --delete-source only unlinks the first volume's source (source.unlink()), orphaning Krieg.part02..partN.rar on disk. When deleting the source for a first-volume archive, also remove its sibling volumes (glob the .partNN.rar set by base name). Add a test. Surfaced alongside ModelTagger2-9nt (PR #47).
close_reason: Built together: delete-source removes all multipart volumes; tqdm progress bar in tagging; corrupt-vs-empty skip-message split + logged extractor error

title: Log retrieval distance + weak-context flag to tagging.log for diagnosability
description:
When a file gets sparse tags (e.g. a blank faction), we currently can't tell from the logs WHY — we have to infer it from token counts and the slug math. Aveline.stl came back with no faction while 'Aveline Plasma no Shield.stl' did, and the only available explanation was inferred (single-token query -> likely unfiltered fallback past the 0.8 weak-context threshold -> model told to ignore lore -> declined faction), not recorded.

Make this diagnosable instead of inferred: in run_tagging (src/tagging.py), include per-file retrieval diagnostics in the tagging.log 'Tagged ...' line (and/or skip lines), specifically:
- best-match distance (distances[0])
- whether the retrieval was slug-filtered vs unfiltered fallback (slug_filtered)
- whether the weak-context note fired (the _WEAK_CONTEXT_DISTANCE guard on the unfiltered path)

So a future 'why did X get no faction?' is answerable straight from tagging.log. Small, log-only change; add a test asserting the new fields appear (the weak-context-fired case is already exercised by test_structured.py).
close_reason: Implemented: tagging.log Tagged line now records dist/slug_filtered/weak_context (combined PR)
